stories.title: Russian attack on Odesa kills three after Ukraine targets Moscow with drones overnight – as it happened
stories.summary: Moscow targets southern city after the Russian capital was hit by 400 drones in response to one of the war’s biggest attacks on Kyiv at the weekend There is also a bit more from Reuters on the context – and the significance – of these continued strikes on ships: “ Both Russia and Ukraine are major grain exporters, and ​the renewed violence on this route has ​raised concerns about supply. Ukraine, which in recent ​seasons accounted for about 6% of global wheat exports and about 11% of global corn

stories.title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
stories.summary: Research during Hungary election shows AI recommended parties not running and gave highly volatile answers to identical prompts AI chatbots provide inaccurate, inconsistent and unreliable guidance to voters asking which party they should back, a study suggests, often recommending the wrong party, not mentioning the right one, or listing parties not even running. “The results raise serious concerns about the reliability of general-purpose AI systems in electoral contexts,” the study , published b

stories.title: Britain’s Brexit Minister Nick Thomas-Symonds axed in reshuffle
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain’s EU Relations Minister Nick Thomas-Symonds is to leave government following Andy Burnham’s reshuffle. The Torfaen MP said in a letter to the new prime minister that he was stepping down “following our conversation.” The close ally of Keir Starmer, who was delivering the prime minister’s EU reset, said Burnham had his “full support.” As Cabinet Office minister, Thomas-Symonds also had responsibility for other policy areas including public inquiries and civil service reform. His

stories.title: Hungary’s chess queen declines Péter Magyar’s offer of role as president
stories.summary: Judit Polgár says she does not ‘feel enough strength within me’ to take on the responsibility of uniting a divided nation The Hungarian chess ⁠legend Judit Polgár has said she will not accept the nomination for president, only a day after the ⁠prime minister, Péter Magyar, ⁠said he ​would ask her to take up the largely ceremonial role. Magyar had said he would meet Polgár on Monday to discuss the matter. “Our country needs unity, peace and a president that all Hungarians can be proud of,” he wro

stories.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
stories.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

stories.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
stories.summary: Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.

stories.title: Vizsgálatot indít a FIFA Argentína ellen Paredes balhéja miatt
stories.summary: A részt vevő játékosokra és edzőkre eltiltás szabható ki.

stories.title: A Fidesz csak súlyosbította a társadalmi bajokat, ennek eredményét látjuk most a budapesti utcákon
stories.summary: Új szereplők jelentek meg a budapesti utcákon: agresszív szerhasználókat, kukákat borogató palackgyűjtögetőket, közterületeken táborozó csapatokat látni, akiktől a fővárosi hajléktalanok is félnek. Az elmúlt korszak társadalmi válsága, a vidéki szegénység mostanra a budapesti középosztály számára is láthatóvá vált: házhoz jött a probléma.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Mivel én is ember vagyok, fogok hibázni, be is vallom, és elnézést fogok érte kérni
stories.summary: Rétvári Bence, a KDNP frakcióvezetője szerint Polgár Judit álllamfővé ajánlása a kormány első komoly politikai kudarca: „ma a legmegosztóbb politikus, akit őt delegál államfőnek, arra átragad ez a megosztottság”. Forsthoffer Ágnes ideiglenes államfő azt mondta: kevesebb indulatra és több kölcsönös tiszteletre van szükség.

stories.title: Áll a bál az Operettszínházban, a társulat nyílt levele szerint elvesztették bizalmukat a vezetőségben
stories.summary: Alacsony anyagi megbecsültségről és a csökkenő szakmai lehetőségekről írnak, az Orbán János Dénessel kapcsolatos feljelentés és vizsgálat szerintük a korábban is jelzett aggályaikat igazolja.

stories.title: Nemrég még Frankfurtban szpottolták Orbán Viktort
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök a Lufthansa járatára várt.

stories.title: Keleten a helyzet megváltozott – élőben a háborúról Rácz Andrással
stories.summary: Egyre durvább orosz csapások, mélységi ukrán dróntámadások az orosz hátországban – az Ukrajnából éppen hazaérkező szakértővel helyi tapasztalatairól, az orosz propagandáról és Szijjártóék Oroszország-politikájáról is beszélgetünk.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerdán bejelenti a Fidesz új frakcióvezetőjét
stories.summary: Sajtótájékoztatót tart.

stories.title: Kaszparov Polgár Judit államfővé ajánlásáról: Reggel írtam Juditnak, ez a lépés igazi vezércsel
stories.summary: A sakkvilág egykori legendája a putyini rezsimet és a hozzá közel álló orbáni rendszert is elutasítja. Szerinte az új magyar kormány egy ilyen jelöléssel fontos üzenetet közvetítene.

stories.title: Nem zavarta az embereket a viharjelzés, 27 embert kellett kimenteni a Balatonból
stories.summary: Viharos volt az idő a tavon a hétvégén, sok dolguk akadt a vízirendőröknek.

stories.title: Spanyolország soha nem látott dominanciával lett világbajnok. És ez még csak a kezdet
stories.summary: A spanyolok erejét a rendszer állandósága adja, a kapitány, De la Fuente már a felnőtt válogatottságuk előtt elkezdett együtt dolgozni a mostani vb-győztes játékosokkal, és ez meg is látszott rajtuk. Az utánpótlás mellett edzőképzésben is élen járnak, úgyhogy az egyetlen kapott góllal megnyert vb és a 38 meccses veretlenségi sorozat után jöhet még csak a java.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem társadalmi konzultációról beszéltünk, hanem azt kértük, hogy küldjenek ajánlásokat
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök kommentben reagált a Polgár Judit jelölése körüli kritikákra, mondván 10 komolyan vehető jelölt érkezett. A sakkozót Radnai Márk is védelmébe vette.

stories.title: Amnesty: Magyar Péter az átgondolatlan kapkodással Polgár Juditot is nehéz helyzetbe hozza
stories.summary: A jogvédő szervezet szerint az Orbán-kormányok idején is egy párt által megválasztott államfő volt, nem kéne ehhez visszatérni.

stories.title: Most van erre harminc napunk – tiszás képviselőket kérdeztünk, hogyan lett ilyen gyorsan Polgár Judit a legalkalmasabb arra, hogy államfőnek jelöljék
stories.summary: Többen is azt mondták, komoly vita volt erről a frakcióban, de aztán nagy egység lett a végére. Az egyik képviselő szerint viszont a kommunikáció nem volt egyértelmű.

stories.title: Guy Ritchie fazonjai és modernkori Kurázsi mama a két új magyar kalandregényben
stories.summary: Fehér Gáspár regénye lúzerekről, Bíró Zsófiáé kemény nőkről szól.

stories.title: Magyar Péter első látványos kudarca – reagált az ellenzék Polgár Judit döntésére
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezető-helyettese, Bóka János szerint a kialakult méltatlan helyzetért a felelősség a miniszterelnököt terheli.

stories.title: Balti bankot vesz az OTP
stories.summary: Az észt, lett és litván piacra is betör a Luminor megvásárlásával a magyar bankcsoport.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a jelölés körüli vitákról és Polgár Judit döntéséről: A felelősség kizárólag engem terhel
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt mondta, sajnálja hogyha a lelkesedéséből fakadó „kommunikációs hibák hozzájárulhattak a kialakult helyzethez” és Polgár Judit döntéséhez. A következő napokban megvitatják az új jelölteket.

stories.title: A Tisza Párt frakciója a jelöltek ismeretében fogja meghozni a döntését, hogy kit támogat államfőjelöltnek
stories.summary: A párt képviselői sajnálják a sakkozó döntését, szerintük minden szempontból alkalmas lett volna köztársasági elnöknek.

stories.title: Hungarian president Tamás Sulyok signs constitutional amendment ending his own mandate
stories.summary: President Tamás Sulyok signed the seventeenth amendment to the Fundamental Law on Saturday, he announced on Facebook. The sitting president’s mandate will end the day after it takes effect.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
stories.summary: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s plans could bring the UK closer to Europe than he realises
stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: ‘It needs to end now’: Democrats urge Trump to halt Iran war after US troop deaths
stories.summary: President accused of having ‘lost control of this war’ as deaths of trio in Iranian military strikes fuel rising concern Democrats have seized on the deaths of three US troops killed in Iranian strikes to urge Donald Trump to urgently reverse his resumption of the war with Iran amid widespread anxiety over climbing casualties. Even as US forces launched a 10th consecutive wave of attacks against Iranian targets, concern over the deaths of American service personnel threatened to complicate moves

stories.title: ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba
stories.summary: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US government As part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers” for Cuba, including Hasan Piker and Amazon Labor Union founder Chris Smalls. In a 100-page report released on Monday, the state department denounces Cuba as the “capital of 21st-century communism”,

stories.summary: Iran has attacked a tanker in the strait of Hormuz, forcing its crew to abandon the ship, after the US launched a tenth consecutive night of strikes Middle East crisis live: follow latest updates Nearly 100 US service members have been injured over the last two weeks of escalating strikes on Iran, the Pentagon has said, as Tehran launched a wave of reprisals to the tenth consecutive night of attacks. stories.title: Climate and rock art advocates take on Woodside’s ‘carbon bomb’ WA gas project in federal court
stories.summary: Albanese government’s approval of North West Shelf gas extension being challenged by the Australian Conservation Foundation and Friends of Australian Rock Art The Albanese government’s decision to approve one of the world’s largest gas projects in Western Australia is being challenged in the federal court, setting up a significant test of how Australia’s environment law deals with the climate crisis. The Australian Conservation Foundation (ACF) and Friends of Australian Rock Art (Fara) are this

stories.title: US issues ‘worldwide caution’ for American citizens – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – our live coverage of the Middle East crisis continues here The price of oil jumped in early Asian trade on Monday after the US carried out its latest wave of strikes on Iran and a vessel caught fire in the strait of Hormuz. Brent crud e for September delivery rose 2.72% to $90.50 a barrel by around 0100 GMT, after briefly crossing $91 – its highest price since June. stories.title: Defence stocks rally as John Healey appointed chancellor; UK borrows less than expected in June – business live
stories.summary: Rolling coverage of the latest economic and financial news And in case you missed it overnight: Donald Trump has said he will impose 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs. The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. This is an important milestone fo

stories.summary: Revolutionary Guard claims attack on oil tanker and two other ships as US carries out tenth consecutive night of strikes Full report: Almost 100 troops injured in recent weeks, Pentagon admits, as Iran launches reprisals on US allies The Jordanian military said it shot down five drones that were launched from Iran, the official Petra news agency reported. It added that the overnight attack did not cause casualties or damage. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: US launches fresh strikes as Trump vows revenge
stories.summary: The US has launched strikes on Iran for a 10th consecutive night as both sides vie for control of the Strait of Hormuz. US President Trump has promised revenge over the deaths of fallen American troops. DW has more.

stories.title: India's 'Cockroaches' reportedly cancel march but maintain protest
stories.summary: India's 'Cockroach' movement protesters have reclaimed their dismantled protest site, a day after police and a sea of youth protesters clashed in Delhi. Organizers however said they are canceling a march. Follow DW.

stories.title: Drone strike sparks major fire in Sumy, northeastern Ukraine
stories.summary: A home improvement shopping centre in Sumy, Ukraine, catches fire after a reported Russian FPV drone strike as firefighters battle the blaze.

stories.title: Trump threatens 50 percent tariff hike on Canadian goods
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday announced that he will impose a 50 percent tariff on some Canadian goods next month, ratcheting up the trade war between the two countries after months of political tension. The administration took three actions using Section 338 of Tariff Act of 1930, a law never before used to impose tariffs, but which the administration says allows it to respond to discriminatory treatment of U.S. products. The tariff hikes, which apply to a range of Canadian exports

stories.title: Germany’s Merz turns resignation of party bigwig to his advantage
stories.summary: BERLIN — The resignation of one of Friedrich Merz’s most important party stalwarts in Berlin is turning into an unexpected political gift for the German chancellor. Jens Spahn, parliamentary leader of Merz’s center-right Christian Democratic Union (CDU), quit on Saturday after he and his husband admitted they used a surrogate mother in the U.S. to become parents, despite the practice being banned in Germany. While the departure initially looked like a setback, Merz moved immediately to tur

stories.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
stories.summary: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

stories.title: EU bets fossil fuel lobby is crying wolf over supply shock warnings
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission is betting that profit-hungry gas and oil companies will continue exporting into Europe next year, resisting calls by companies to rewrite new climate laws that they say will force them to seek other markets. Fossil fuel companies and member countries say new EU rules requiring them to track emissions of methane — a potent greenhouse gas — beginning in 2027 will be impossible to comply with in time, diverting vast chunks of the EU’s supply as exporters strain t

stories.title: United Airlines says it will ‘look into’ worker’s threat to call ICE in viral video
stories.summary: United staffer told customer ‘you don’t act like at a citizen’ during incident over wait time at San Francisco airport United Airlines is “looking into” a viral video showing a United Airlines employee threatening to call Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) on Mexican American man at San Francisco international Airport last week. The scene erupted after Julio Varela of San Ramon grew upset at United Airlines staff for taking a long time to correct an error his family made when entering h

stories.title: ‘A partnership between settlers and the army’: two shot dead in latest West Bank raid
stories.summary: Residents of Deir Jarir say raids late on Sunday, also involving security forces, are part of pattern of violence Even by the brutal standards of militant settler violence in the West Bank, the raid on Deir Jarir late on Sunday night that killed two Palestinians and left another in hospital with a gunshot wound was extreme. Palestinians in the town said settlers from a nearby outpost arrived after 9pm on Sunday claiming that they were searching for stolen livestock. Israeli soldiers and police s

stories.title: Andy Burnham to cut VAT on electricity bills as first cost of living move
stories.summary: Newly appointed chancellor John Healey says reduction will be funded by cancelling digital ID scheme Electricity bills will be reduced by an average of £45 a year from October, after Andy Burnham announced the government would remove VAT from them. The new prime minister said on Tuesday he was implementing an immediate tax cut to help ease cost of living pressures for households in Britain. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australia news live: Hanson’s support for Trump deportation policy ‘makes me sick’, Monique Ryan says; NT traditional owners rally outside Pine Gap base
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Alpine ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria saw climate records shattered at the weekend with maximum temperatures up to 12C above average. Ski areas of Mount Hotham and Falls Creek set July records for their hottest daytime and night-time temperatures and Thredbo also set a new hottest July day, according to the Bureau of Meteorology. We want to see more Australian ideas commercialised, more Australian

stories.title: Burnham cuts VAT on electricity bills in first major policy announcement – UK politics live
stories.summary: Move is expected to save households around £45 a year in effort to tackle cost of living Burnham to cut VAT on electricity bills in first cost-of-living move The newly appointed business secretary, Jonathan Reynolds, has defended the policy, declaring the cut to VAT on electricity bills “a statement of priorities”. Responding to Darren Jones’s claim that the cut remained unfunded, Reynolds told Radio 4’s Today programme: They are part of the government’s pressure, it is a legitimate decision to

stories.title: Tens of thousands of Cockroach movement protesters defy police with Delhi march
stories.summary: Youth-led group calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in years Tens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years. A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already

stories.title: Canada issues air quality warnings over US wildfire smoke after Trump tariff threat
stories.summary: Officials say smoke from US fires has crossed border days after US president accused Canada of improper fire management After days of acrid smoke from northern wildfires choking major US cities and inspiring Donald Trump to threaten Canada with tariffs, officials now say the reverse is also happening as parts of Canada issue air quality warnings caused by American fires to the south. Officials in the Canadian province of British Columbia warned in an alert on Saturday evening that smoke from US

stories.title: Ukraine’s army chief expected to lose job after week of protests
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s chief of staff hints government may be about to sack Oleksandr Syrskyi Ukraine’s commander in chief Oleksandr Syrskyi is widely expected to lose his job amid signs that Volodymyr Zelenskyy has bowed to pressure from protesters and will reshuffle the army’s top leadership. Over the past five days, thousands of demonstrators have gathered outside the presidential office in Kyiv, chanting: “Syrskyi out.” They have demanded Zelenskyy reinstate Mykhailo Fedorov, who was fired as

stories.title: UK's new PM Andy Burnham cuts household electricity tax
stories.summary: The UK's new leader moved to remove VAT from electricity bills from October 1. The move comes before Burnham chairs his first cabinet meeting later today.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz to host Azerbaijan President Aliyev
stories.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev will focus on energy and Armenia. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children. DW has more.

stories.title: At least 27 dead and 83 missing after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
stories.summary: An investigation into the tragedy has been launched, the island's prime minister says, adding he hopes more will be found alive.

stories.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US strikes Iran for tenth consecutive night
stories.summary: As fighting escalates between both sides, Iran's interior minister travelled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.

stories.title: Why are Ukraine's military recruiters facing violence in their own country?
stories.summary: The latest National Police Service report recorded more than 600 assaults on recruitment officers to date and warned that the aggression has become “increasingly threatening”.

stories.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
stories.summary: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

stories.title: Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
stories.summary: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.

stories.title: Wes Streeting named to top UK defense role
stories.summary: LONDON — Wes Streeting will become the U.K.’s new defense secretary , Downing Street announced, as Andy Burnham unveiled his new Cabinet after becoming prime minister. Streeting take over less than six weeks after predecessor Dan Jarvis, who was appointed amid a fierce row within government over defense spending, was named to the job by Burnham’s predecessor Keir Starmer. Jarvis was responsible for delivering the government’s long-awaited Defence Investment Plan, which allocated money to t

stories.title: Nicaragua’s president says country will not hold any more elections
stories.summary: Daniel Ortega, the 80-year-old authoritarian leader, says move will ‘build a wall’ against the opposition Nicaragua’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has said the country will hold no further elections to prevent the opposition from coming to power. The 80-year-old authoritarian leader, who has been in office since 2007, also said he would work with the congress he controls to pass new laws that would “build a wall” against the opposition. Continue reading...

stories.title: Five people die in attempt to save struggling swimmer in Ohio river
stories.summary: Rescue crews find two women, pronounced dead at hospital, and the bodies of three men after motorist calls 911 Five people have died in a river in Ohio after several of them went into the water to help another person who was struggling to swim, authorities said Monday. A motorist called 911 after spotting a frightened child who had been with the group and was running on a nearby road, Delaware county sheriff Jeffrey Balzer said at a news conference on Monday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tropical Storm Bertha heads for land as Hurricane Fausto forms in eastern Pacific
stories.summary: Bertha could bring heavy rain on US Gulf coast, with its center located 110 miles south of Panama City, Florida Tropical Storm Bertha formed Monday evening in the Gulf of Mexico south of the Florida Panhandle as it drifts erratically toward populated coastal areas, bringing a threat of heavy rain, storm surge and damaging wind gusts. Meanwhile, in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Hurricane Fausto formed far from Mexico’s Baja California peninsula and posed no threat to land Monday night. Continue read

stories.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
stories.summary: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy products Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products. stories.title: Man of his word: Pope Leo speeches declared human-authored by Australian AI detection tool
stories.summary: Maps of Hope has been certified by Proudly Human, a company led by former chief scientist, Dr Alan Finkel Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A collection of speeches and writings by Pope Leo XIV has been certified as human-authored less than two months after Leo declared artificial intelligence the greatest threat to humanity. stories.title: UK employers cut job vacancies as Burnham aims to lift living standards
stories.summary: Unemployment holds steady at 4.9%, underlining the challenge facing the new prime minister Business live – latest updates Employers cut the number of job vacancies in May, according to official figures that highlight the UK’s “fragile” economic outlook amid the conflict in the Middle East. Job vacancies fell to 712,000 – almost half the level in 2022 – as employers put off hiring new staff in the three months to May, the Office for National Statistics said. Continue reading...

stories.title: JP Morgan boss warns of ‘consequences’ if Burnham taxes banks
stories.summary: Higher taxes on sector could threaten plan for £3bn headquarters in London, says Jamie Dimon Business live – latest updates The boss of JP Morgan, Jamie Dimon, has warned Andy Burnham against raising tax charges on banks, suggesting it could threaten plans to build its £3bn headquarters in London and drive investment away from Britain. The chief executive of the world’s biggest bank, who has a track record of criticising the UK’s bank tax surcharge , does not want to see the UK’s new prime minis

stories.title: Out of Sight review – mountain idyll in the Austrian uplands is a gilded cage for distressed asylum seekers
stories.summary: Young men share their heartbreaking experiences of an Alpine ‘deportation camp’ in Lisa Polster’s impactful, gentle documentary Located in the Austrian Tyrol, the Bürglkopf mountain is a picture-perfect Alpine landscape, with its verdant fields and scenic trails. The tranquil idyll is a popular holiday spot, with thousands of tourists each year. Some visitors, however, are less welcome than others. stories.title: US imposes new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods
stories.summary: The tariffs will cover products including wine and hockey sticks, but not energy or fish. The move is the latest trade escalation between the US and its northern neighbor under the Trump administration.

stories.title: Tate brothers to fight extradition to UK, says lawyer
stories.summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate's lawyer said that the brothers are victims of a "political witch hunt." He has vowed to challenge their extradition to the UK.

stories.title: Why Japan's new imperial law keeps women off the throne
stories.summary: New legislation aims to address the shrinking ranks of Japan's imperial family, while preserving male-only succession despite broad support for a female emperor.

stories.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
stories.summary: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia

stories.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?

stories.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for failing to stop sale of illegal and fake goods
stories.summary: Chinese retail platform handed penalty over listings for items such as counterfeit products and unsafe toys Business live – latest updates The Chinese online retail platform AliExpress has been fined a record €550m (£470m) by the EU over its failure to stop illegal goods including harmful clothing, cosmetics and kitchen gadgets being sold through its site. The European Commission fine is the biggest imposed by the bloc under Digital Services Act (DSA) legislation to protect consumers from illega

stories.title: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in prison in US federal court
stories.summary: Case against Ismael Zambada García, who helped co-found and lead the violent Sinaloa cartel, unfolded in New York The Mexican drug kingpin, Ismael Zambada García, known as “El Mayo”, was sentenced in a US federal court on Monday to life in prison, after a plea deal last year in which he admitted ⁠to shipping vast amounts of cocaine and other substances as leader of the violent Sinaloa cartel. Zambada is 76 and one of the most high-profile Mexican ⁠drug traffickers to be sentenced in a US court s

stories.title: Afghanistan floods kill at least 20 people, with more than 100 missing
stories.summary: National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in remote province of Nuristan At least 20 people have been killed and more than 80 injured in flooding that hit Afghanistan’s Nuristan province, the country’s disaster management agency said on Monday. In Parun, the provincial capital, a further 105 people including the mayor were missing, said Feraidon Samim, a spokesperson for the Nuristan governor. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
stories.summary: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

stories.title: DRC's neighbors boost preparedness as Ebola cases surge
stories.summary: Uganda, Rwanda and Zambia are strengthening border screening, disease surveillance and emergency response measures. The growing Ebola outbreak in the DRC is raising concerns about cross-border spread.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: US ties to Israel rattle rare earth deal in Malaysia
stories.summary: Malaysia's rare earth refinery in Gebeng is essential to a strategic deal between the Pentagon and Australia's Lynas mining corporation. Now, Malaysian activists are using it as leverage to support Palestinians in Gaza.

stories.title: Guterres to visit Cyprus as UN seeks to revive peace talks
stories.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

stories.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine

stories.summary: The Iran-backed Houthis declared a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia after accusing the kingdom of attacking Sana'a airport and maintaining a years-long blockade, putting one of the world's busiest shipping lanes at risk.

stories.title: Rare 48C heatwave forces Uzbekistan to adapt to extreme temperatures
stories.summary: Authorities slowed metro trains, restricted heavy vehicles and closed kindergartens as temperatures climbed to 48C in parts of the country during an unusually intense heatwave.

stories.title: ‘All I think about is her’: Families demand answers as Nigeria's boat disasters keep happening
stories.summary: Hundreds of lives are lost each year in Nigeria as a result of boat accidents yet nothing seems to change.

stories.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
stories.summary: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

stories.title: Merger deal between Paramount and Warner Bros paused by judge

stories.title: Trump utasításba adta, hogy valahányszor Irán megöl egy amerikait, a „sokszorosát fizessék meg”

stories.summary: A baleset a Platán strand sétányán történt szombat este, a fiú nem viselt bukósisakot.

stories.summary: Egyelőre csak 14 napra.

stories.summary: Sajnos kiadósabb csapadék sem várható, mindössze néhány helyen lehetnek záporok, zivatarok.

The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and d

stories.title: ‘When I get home, my whole body aches’: extreme heat saps health and wages of India’s poorest workers
stories.summary: Exclusive: Outdoor workers losing up to 9% of income as weather becomes ‘poverty multiplier’, research says Extreme heat is pushing some of India’s poorest workers further into poverty, with income losses of up to 9% for outdoor workers, new research has found. Analysis done by the International Institute for Environment and Development (IIED), and shared exclusively with the Guardian, found that extreme heat is acting as a “poverty multiplier” for hundreds of millions of workers in India’s info

stories.title: Changing face of the Peloponnese: backdrop to The Odyssey braces for tourism surge
stories.summary: Tracing a journey across the Peloponnese, taking as its starting point the locations of Christopher Nolan’s new film. As the region faces mounting tourism and development pressures, the environment is becoming ever more fragile under the strain of recurring wildfires and demand for natural resources, particularly water Voidokilia Bay, in the southwestern Peloponnese, has a crescent-shaped beach framed by two historical headlands: a Mycenaean tomb on one side and a medieval fortress on the other.

stories.title: Nagy Tímea nem fideszesként és nem tiszásként ajánlotta Polgár Judit államfői jelölését
stories.summary: A korábban az egyik digitális polgári körhöz csatlakozó olimpiai bajnok párbajtőröző indította el azt a levelet, amelyben tizenhat ismert személyiség a sakknagymestert javasolta köztársasági elnöknek. Beszéltünk a levél több aláírójával, köztük Mácsai Pállal, aki úgy látja: egy maszkulin berendezkedésű rendszer után jó lenne, ha az elnök egy anya szemével látná az országot.

stories.title: Polgár Judit: A felkérésnek nem tudok eleget tenni
stories.summary: Megköszönte, de visszautasította a köztársasági elnöki tisztségre érkezett felkérést. Azt írta, nem érez magában erőt, hogy magára vállalja egy megosztott nemzet egyesítésének történelmi felelősségét, de egy új, független és elismert köztársasági elnököt támogat majd e célok elérésében.

stories.title: Török Gábor: Ha a hatalompolitika nyelvén Sulyok Tamás eltávolítása siker volt, akkor ez bizony az új kormány első kudarca
stories.summary: A politikai elemző arra reagált, hogy Polgár Judit visszautasította a miniszterelnök felkérését a köztársasági elnöki tisztségre.

stories.title: Az angol Botka László tíz évre tervez, negyven éve nem látott változásokat ígér, de Trump rögtön próbára teheti
stories.summary: Nem politikai nézeteivel, hanem személyiségével hozhat változást a hétfőn beiktatott új brit miniszterelnök, Andy Burnham. Történelmi mélypontról kéne elmozdítania a brit baloldalt, és meg kell győznie a szavazókat, hogy ő valóban a nép embere, ahogy mondja. Türelmesek nem lesznek vele, miközben konkrét terveiről alig tudni valamit.

stories.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
stories.summary: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

stories.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
stories.summary: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

stories.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
stories.summary: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

stories.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
stories.summary: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

stories.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition
stories.summary: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami ahead of an extradition request from the United Kingdom. British authorities accuse them of serious offences, including rape, human trafficking and sexual exploitation.

stories.title: Passenger ferry carrying 116 passengers sinks off the coast of Guyana
stories.summary: A distress call by the MV Barima to the coast guard was made just after 11pm local time. The vessel is said to be equipped with 250 life jackets, two rigid life boats and six inflatable life crafts.

stories.title: Magnitude 5.1 earthquake in central Peru leaves at least five dead and ten injured
stories.summary: A 5.1‑magnitude earthquake centred in Chupaca, Junín, left five people dead and around ten injured on Saturday night, and damaged hospitals and religious monuments in the Andean area.

stories.title: Trump and Sánchez meet at World Cup final after nearly two years of diplomatic tensions
stories.summary: For nearly two years, the leaders of Spain and the United States have clashed over issues ranging from defence spending to US policy in the Middle East.

stories.title: Watch: Torrential rain hits New York and New Jersey triggering flash floods
stories.summary: Water cascaded into subway stations and flooded streets, with people seen wading through water.

stories.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
stories.summary: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.

stories.title: Watch: Spain fans erupt in celebration after World Cup final victory

stories.title: This farmer wanted to quit the cocaine industry - he couldn't

stories.summary: Thousands of spectators watched Spain's victory in central Madrid on late Sunday night.

stories.title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
stories.summary: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.

stories.title: US hits Iran for ninth night as Khamenei calls ceasefire 'worthless'
stories.summary: US Central Command said it targeted military command centres, air defence systems and missile sites overnight, as Iran's ayatollah called Trump's signature on the interim deal "worthless and invalid" and a negotiator said Tehran was suspending its commitments.

stories.title: Spanish and Argentinian fans react outside World Cup final
stories.summary: Ecstatic Spanish spectators and disappointed Argentine fans spoke to the BBC after the World Cup final on Sunday.

stories.title: Trump and Infantino hand World Cup trophy to Spain
stories.summary: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino hand the World Cup trophy to Spain captain Rodri after Spain beat Argentina in the final near New York.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup victory
stories.summary: Spain fans celebrate their World Cup final victory over Argentina in Times Square, New York, waving flags, chanting and lifting a replica trophy.

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
stories.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

stories.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
stories.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

stories.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
stories.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

stories.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
stories.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

stories.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
stories.summary: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

stories.title: Deaths from Venezuela earthquakes rise above 5,000
stories.summary: Venezuela has been able to unlock €302 million of funding from the International Monetary Fund to support recovery and reconstruction following the devastating earthquakes that hit the northern coastal region last month.

stories.title: US launches new wave of strikes on Iran as Israel vows 'powerful' response to Tehran
stories.summary: Iran says the latest US strikes killed at least 50 people and wounded 517. Washington said the attacks were retaliation for a strike on a US base in Jordan that killed two US service members, left one missing and injured four others.

stories.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
stories.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

stories.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.title: England beat France 6-4 to claim World Cup third place
stories.summary: Bukayo Saka scored a hat trick as England beat France 6-4 in a record-breaking World Cup third-place playoff. The Three Lions survived a dramatic French comeback to secure their best finish at the tournament since winning the title in 1966.

stories.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
stories.summary: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Conti

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Trump, not Iran, is the world’s greatest danger. He’s a one-man weapon of mass destruction | Simon Tisdall
stories.summary: As the bombing starts again, it’s clear the president has dragged the US into a limitless fiasco – and the world into an economic quagmire Feckless and clueless, Donald Trump is lost in Iran, unable to find a way out of the disastrous war he started. Once again, the US military is pummelling the country and, increasingly, its civilian infrastructure. As before, this unlawful bludgeoning strengthens the resistance of a hardline regime that cares little for its people’s suffering. How often have T

stories.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
stories.summary: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
stories.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

stories.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
stories.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
stories.summary: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June. The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
stories.summary: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca J

stories.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
stories.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

stories.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
stories.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

stories.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
stories.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

stories.title: Watch: US Navy Blue Angels' low flyover leaves beachgoers stunned
stories.summary: US Navy Blue Angels jets flew low over a crowded Florida beach, startling beachgoers as chairs and tents were blown away and children covered their ears. The dramatic flyover sparked renewed debate over the safety of low-altitude military demonstrations.

stories.title: Euronews aboard EU warships in the Red Sea: Europe eyes wider mandate as Houthi threat grows
stories.summary: In a special report from EU patrol ships in Djibouti, Kaja Kallas told Euronews that the 27-member bloc was discussing expanding Aspides' mandate and that ships pledged to a Hormuz coalition could be redeployed to the Red Sea in the interim.

stories.title: US forces strike Iran for seventh consecutive night
stories.summary: Iran's Fars state news agency reported that Iranian forces had responded by attacking US allies and bases in the region, including the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait.

stories.title: Woman speaks out on husband being pulled from Ryanair plane – video
stories.summary: Svetlana Grković and her husband, Ljubisa Karović, had just settled into a Ryanair flight last week when a loud bang pierced the hum of engines. Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked out of the plane through a dislodged cabin window. Karovic is now being treated in hospital in Thessaloniki with severe neck and arm injuries, Maksimovic said ‘If we die, we die together’: wife of Ryanair passenger almost sucked through window speaks Continue reading...

stories.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
stories.summary: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organiz

stories.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
stories.summary: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

stories.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
stories.summary: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
stories.summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AfD party.

stories.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
stories.summary: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

stories.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
stories.summary: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

stories.title: Germany may make it illegal to deny Israel's right to exist
stories.summary: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

stories.title: German tenants need better heat protection
stories.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

stories.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

stories.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.
